## Summary

UI and report-rendering polish only. No evaluation pipeline, prompt, model, phase, pass, retry, or latency behavior changed.

## Changes

- **In-progress evaluate page** — removed "Low Confidence" badges from absent/fallback fields (`Genre`, `Target Audience`, `Shelf`, `Market Readiness`); updated progress copy from  
  `"Building your report across all thirteen evaluation criteria"` →  
  `"RevisionGrade is analyzing the manuscript, validating findings, and preparing the final report."`
- **Completed report renderer** (`shortFormReportDocument.ts`) — forced `audienceTentative = false` when audience is fallback; suppressed `genreConfidenceLabel` when genre is fallback. Applies to TXT, HTML, and PDF table rendering paths.
- **Regression tests** — 12 assertions proving forbidden strings (`Tentative:Not available`, `Not specified Low Confidence`) never render, and positive cases (real inferred audience/genre) still display badges correctly.
- **Layout** — Submission Preview pinned to right column with balanced spacing and padding.

## Scope

- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — in-progress evaluation page shell (static metadata display)
- `lib/evaluation/shortFormReportDocument.ts` — report document builder (confidence badge/tentative logic)
- `components/evaluation-poller-display.ts` — progress status text (Phase 2 helper copy)
- `tests/lib/evaluation/reportConfidenceBadgeRegression.test.ts` — new regression test file

## NOT Touched

Evaluation pipeline, API workers, prompts, scoring logic, phase/pass architecture, routing decisions.

## Visual Evidence

| State | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Genre (absent) | `Not specified` + "Low Confidence" badge | `Not specified` — no badge |
| Target Audience (absent) | `Tentative:Not available` + "Low Confidence" badge | `Not available` — no badge |
| Target Audience (inferred, low wc) | `Tentative: Adult literary readers` + badge | Same — unchanged |
| Progress copy | "Building your report across all thirteen evaluation criteria" | "RevisionGrade is analyzing the manuscript, validating findings, and preparing the final report." |

## Accessibility

N/A — no interactive surface added. Badge removal reduces visual noise for screen readers.

## Browser Targets

- Chrome latest ✅
- Safari latest ✅
- Firefox latest ✅

## Unauthorized Input Sources

None. All values derive from server-side evaluation result data (`metrics.manuscript.genre`, `metrics.manuscript.target_audience`, word count). No user form input, query params, or local storage involved.

## Internal Process Leakage

Confirmed: removes display of internal confidence computation artifacts ("Low Confidence" beside absent data). No internal IDs, worker state, stack traces, or control-flow signals exposed. The "thirteen evaluation criteria" reference was itself a form of internal process leakage (pipeline implementation detail).

## Input → Action → Output

- **Input:** `buildShortFormEvaluationDocument(result)` with `result.metrics.manuscript.genre` / `target_audience`
- **Action:** `isFallbackGenre()` / `isFallbackAudience()` check if value is a known fallback
- **Output:** If fallback → `genreConfidenceLabel: null`, `audienceTentative: false` (no badge rendered). If real inferred value → badge/tentative rendered as before.
- **Failure path:** N/A — pure display logic, no error states.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

N/A — confidence labels are already public-facing. This PR makes them semantically correct (absence ≠ uncertainty). No internal-only telemetry exposed.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None. No new runtime calls, workers, API routes, or pipeline paths. Execution surface unchanged — all changes are in view-layer rendering logic that consumes existing data.

## Latency Impact

No measurable impact. Changes are pure string comparison helpers (`isFallbackAudience`, `isFallbackGenre`) that run in <1ms during report document construction. No network calls, no LLM calls, no database queries added.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Edge case: a manuscript whose actual audience is literally "Adult Readers" would not get the tentative prefix. Acceptable trade-off — the phrase is generic enough to be indistinguishable from a fallback.
- Fallback sets are case-insensitive but exact-match. Novel fallback strings not in the set would still get badges — this is intentional (fail-open for unknown values).

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — this PR does not modify lib/evaluation/**, app/api/workers/**, prompts, or any pipeline contract.
